title: The Battle Over Trump’s Immigration Crackdown Comes to a Newark ICE Facility Parking Lot
description: For five days, activists have gathered outside a detention center in New Jersey to denounce conditions inside. Federal officials have rejected those concerns.
author: Ana Ley and Mark Bonamo

title: Why the Ebola and Hantavirus Outbreaks Have Confounded Scientists
description: The types of Ebola and hantavirus worrying officials are very different from the species identified decades ago, raising new questions about how to respond.
author: Carl Zimmer

title: Writing Is Fundamental to How We Think
description: A.I. can be a crutch that hurts our ability to think creatively.
author: Rebecca Winthrop

title: Reflecting Pool Contract Has ‘Inflated’ Profit Margin, Government Analysis Finds
description: The firm that was given a no-bid contract to fix the troubled landmark is charging 20 percent. The typical profit margin is 6 percent to 12 percent, internal records show.
author: David A. Fahrenthold

title: America’s Last Surviving Wallace Neff Bubble House Is for Sale
description: Located in Pasadena, Calif., this futuristic dome — and affordable housing prototype — still stuns.
author: Linda Laban

title: What Five Years in Big Law at WilmerHale Taught Me
description: Despite all our good intentions, the legal system — that thing we were charged to protect — kept getting worse.
author: Matthew Wollin

title: High-Level British Spy Warns of Expanding Russia Threat
description: The director of Britain’s electronic surveillance agency warned that Moscow was getting more brazen, adding that estimates suggested 500,000 Russian soldiers had been killed in the Ukraine war.
author: Adam Goldman

title: Cubans Deported to Mexico Live a Precarious Existence, Report Finds
description: A Human Rights Watch report found that many Cubans deported to Mexico by the Trump administration are living in an “indefinite legal limbo” and struggling to get by.
author: Patricia Mazzei

title: A Quiet Pacific Village Becomes China’s Security Testing Ground
description: When a remote Pacific village asked for help with rowdy youth, the Chinese police arrived with a surveillance system. Then came the backlash.
author: David Pierson, Berry Wang and Adam Ferguson

title: Court Rejects Alabama House Map, Calling It Unfair to Black Voters
description: Alabama is likely to appeal the ruling, which stops an effort to use a new congressional map that would likely cost Democrats a majority-Black district.
author: Emily Cochrane and Abbie VanSickle

title: Teachers Union Urges Schools to Curb A.I. Chatbots and Screen Time
description: The American Federation of Teachers recommended “no screens” at all for those in second grade or younger, and no A.I. chatbots for students in elementary school.
author: Natasha Singer

title: Time Gave Me Back What Prison Took Away
description: A formerly incarcerated writer reflects on how the prison system didn’t foster change, but befriending time did.
author: Marvin Wade and Evan Bode

title: Endorsed by Trump, Paxton Crushes Cornyn: Takeaways From the Texas Runoff Elections
description: President Trump’s record of ousting those he sees as disloyal continued apace with Senator John Cornyn’s defeat. Whether his relationship with Senate Republicans can be repaired is another question.
author: Shane Goldmacher

title: A Blue Texas May Be More Than a Dream for Democrats
description: Ken Paxton’s victory for the Republican nomination and a big shift among Hispanic voters have put a Senate seat within reach.
author: Nate Cohn

title: Sonny Rollins, Giant of the Jazz Saxophone, Is Dead at 95
description: Even by the standards of a music that prizes individuality, he stood out, as both a musician and a personality.
author: Peter Keepnews

title: A Fiery Visitor From Space Photo-Bombs an Erupting Volcano
description: For a split second, a fireball outshone the rivers of molten lava flowing from the Philippines’ most active volcano.
author: Robin George Andrews

title: Trump Administration to Send Americans Exposed to Ebola to Kenya
description: In past outbreaks, Americans exposed to the virus were sent home to be treated in state-of-the-art facilities. The Trump administration has already flown some U.S. citizens to Europe for treatment.
author: Apoorva Mandavilli and Zolan Kanno-Youngs

title: Supreme Court Reverses Ruling in Immigration Judges’ Free Speech Lawsuit
description: A group of immigration judges in 2020 challenged work-related restrictions on their public speaking engagements, saying they violated their free speech rights.
author: Ann E. Marimow

title: U.S. Aims to Give Cold War Plutonium to Start-Ups for Nuclear Fuel
description: Companies say it’s a better way to dispose of the Cold War-era material — and fix a shortage of nuclear fuel. But the plan has also faced criticism from nonproliferation experts.
author: Brad Plumer

title: Trump Declares Himself in Good Health After Physical Exam at Walter Reed
description: President Trump, who will turn 80 next month, did not give details on the exam.
author: Karoun Demirjian
